source_note: Original TOEIC-style workplace simulation content generated for this application. Not official test-provider material.

speaking.title: Read Aloud 1
speaking.prompt_text: Good morning, everyone. The executive briefing room on the fourth floor will be unavailable on August 9 while the facilities team recalibrates the hybrid presentation system and replaces several ceiling microphones. Please redirect scheduled meetings to Conference Room B and notify external guests in advance.
speaking.sample_response: Good morning, everyone. The executive briefing room on the fourth floor will be unavailable on August 9 while the facilities team recalibrates the hybrid presentation system and replaces several ceiling microphones. Please redirect scheduled meetings to Conference Room B and notify external guests in advance.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.

speaking.title: Read Aloud 2
speaking.prompt_text: Thank you for calling Meridian Health Systems. Our service desk is open from eight thirty in the morning until six in the evening, Monday through Friday. For urgent delivery discrepancies, please leave your reference number, destination branch, and preferred callback time after the tone.
speaking.sample_response: Thank you for calling Meridian Health Systems. Our service desk is open from eight thirty in the morning until six in the evening, Monday through Friday. For urgent delivery discrepancies, please leave your reference number, destination branch, and preferred callback time after the tone.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.

speaking.title: Describe Picture 3
speaking.prompt_text: Describe the picture on the screen in as much detail as possible.
speaking.sample_response: The picture shows a cross-functional team reviewing operational documents in a modern office area. One employee appears to be clarifying data on a shared screen while another compares printed materials, suggesting that they are preparing for a detailed business discussion.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_note: No prompt audio: this TOEIC SW task uses on-screen text or picture stimulus.
speaking.image_prompt:
Photorealistic TOEIC SW C2-hard workplace stimulus, 4:3.
Context: urban rail service recovery.
Scene: close-up of a large professional worktable with one simple shelf behind it; no room-wide view. Task: hard picture-description still-life scene with separated priority items, conflicting color groups, and an implied delay. Include enough concrete visual clues for priority, spatial relationship, implied risk, and workplace-problem description without any writing.
Props: radio headsets on hooks, dark blank control panels, smooth storage modules, plain barrier strips, and locked equipment drawers. Arrange them as a realistic priority conflict with clear spatial relationships.
Camera: medium-close documentary photograph, eye-level view, natural indoor light, tight crop on table and shelf only, no visible ceiling or signage, layered foreground/background, sharp realistic materials.
Strict exclusions: no people, reflections, text, letters, numbers, labels, logos, watermarks, signs, maps, clocks, chairs, paper, folders, cardboard, pallet jacks, cones, carts, wheels, vehicles, aircraft, machinery, pipes, ceiling, doors, windows, or screens. Blank smooth surfaces only.
Avoid cartoon/vector style, warped geometry, fake signage, surreal objects, melted handles, malformed hinges, and nonfunctional details.
speaking.image_quality_target: photorealistic C2-hard TOEIC SW workplace scene; no readable text, logos, watermarks, or illustration style

speaking.title: Respond to Question 5
speaking.prompt_text: How do you judge whether a professional presentation contains enough evidence?
speaking.sample_response: A strong response answers directly, adds a specific reason, and uses clear workplace vocabulary.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

Directions: Answer the following question.

How do you judge whether a professional presentation contains enough evidence?

speaking.title: Respond to Question 7
speaking.prompt_text: Should a company prioritize long-term trust over short-term revenue when a client requests an unrealistic timeline? Why or why not?
speaking.sample_response: A strong response answers directly, adds a specific reason, and uses clear workplace vocabulary.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

speaking.title: Use Provided Information 8
speaking.prompt_text: When does the tolerance-standard comparison begin?
speaking.information_card:
Quality Assurance Calibration
Venue: Meridian Health Systems inspection center
8:40-9:15 Sample intake and labeling review
9:30-10:25 Tolerance-standard comparison in Lab 4
10:40-11:35 Defect-escalation practice with senior inspectors
13:00-14:05 Supplier feedback meeting in Conference Room C
14:20-15:00 Corrective-action deadline confirmation
speaking.sample_response: A strong response uses only the schedule information and answers the caller clearly.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

speaking.title: Express an Opinion
speaking.prompt_text: Some businesses invest heavily in customer acquisition, while others focus on retaining current customers through better service. Which investment is more valuable in a competitive market? Give reasons and examples.
speaking.sample_response: A strong response states a clear preference, gives two developed reasons, and uses a concrete workplace example.
speaking.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.
speaking.audio_loudness: loud literal TTS with provider-specific normalization
speaking.audio_script:
Directions: Express your opinion and support it with reasons and examples.

Some businesses invest heavily in customer acquisition, while others focus on retaining current customers through better service. Which investment is more valuable in a competitive market? Give reasons and examples.
speaking.audio_transcript:
Directions: Express your opinion and support it with reasons and examples.

Some businesses invest heavily in customer acquisition, while others focus on retaining current customers through better service. Which investment is more valuable in a competitive market? Give reasons and examples.

writing.title: Picture Sentence 1
writing.prompt_text: Write one sentence based on the picture. Use both required words or phrases. You may change the form of the words and use them in any order.
writing.sample_response: The manager is reviewing the updated schedule with her team.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For writing, include sentence accuracy, content coverage, tone, and development.

Context: urban rail service recovery.

writing.title: Respond to a client request
writing.prompt_text: You received an email from a client who wants to move a product demonstration from August 9 to the following week because their procurement committee added a compliance review. Write a response that acknowledges the request, explains what you need to check, and offers a practical next step.
writing.sample_response: A strong email response addresses every requested point, uses a professional tone, and ends with a clear next step.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

writing.title: Respond to an internal request
writing.prompt_text: Your manager has asked whether the facilities team can prepare a concise update for next Friday's staff meeting after several regional targets were revised. Write a response that confirms availability, mentions one topic you will include, and asks one practical question.
writing.sample_response: A strong email response addresses every requested point, uses a professional tone, and ends with a clear next step.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For speaking, include fluency, pronunciation evidence, intelligibility, and coherence.

writing.title: Opinion Essay
writing.prompt_text: Some people believe that employees learn best by working with experienced coworkers. Others believe formal training courses are more effective because they create consistent standards across a company. Which view do you agree with? Give reasons and examples to support your opinion.
writing.sample_response: A strong essay states a clear position, develops at least two reasons, includes workplace examples, and uses organized paragraphs.
writing.scoring_rubric: C2 target: score task completion, nuance, relevance, organization, grammar control, vocabulary range, idiomatic precision, and clarity. For writing, include sentence accuracy, content coverage, tone, and development.
